opportunities.title: Godzilla-Sized Ruling: How to Actually Run a Kaiju in 5e Without Melting the Table
opportunities.why_now: EN World's podcast just teased a Godzilla RPG tie-in, giving a hook to break down giant-creature mechanics in mainline 5e.
opportunities.angle: 60-sec 'creature tactic' short: convert a kaiju stat block using legendary actions + lair effects, show the one ruling that keeps it from one-shotting the party.

opportunities.title: The One Background Rule Everyone Houserules Wrong
opportunities.why_now: Arcana Unleashed's new background reveal just dropped, making backgrounds a hot topic right now.
opportunities.angle: Dry rules-ruling short: pick the most-abused background feature (e.g., Guild Artisan free lodging) and show the RAW limit vs. the common misplay.

opportunities.title: Free Adventure Autopsy: Is This Week's EN World Freebie Actually Runnable?
opportunities.why_now: The July 19 freebies/sales roundup just posted, giving a fresh, time-limited product to react to before the sale window closes.
opportunities.angle: Quick verdict format: grab one free module from the list, show one encounter's stat block, flag the one thing you'd fix before running it tonight.

opportunities.title: Why 'Kid-Friendly Mayhem' Still Needs a Death Save
opportunities.why_now: EN World's 'Spoonful of Sugar' piece on softening mayhem for younger tables is fresh and lets you take a hard mechanical stance against fluffing consequences.
opportunities.angle: Wry GM-veteran take: show how to keep stakes real (failed saves, resource drain) while trimming gore — one concrete house rule, not a vibe.

opportunities.title: Sly Flourish's Adventure Types, Graded: Which One Actually Saves You Prep Time
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish just published a fresh breakdown of adventure types from Rise of the Lazy Gamemaster, a core reference for prep-lite GMs.
opportunities.angle: Mechanical comparison short: pick two adventure types from the article, run the math on prep-minutes-saved vs. player agency lost.
